key_insight: Molecular MPNN performance is driven more by how messages are constructed than by how complex the node update is, with message-seed initialization and node-edge fusion mattering most.
engineering_relevance: The study gives practical design rules for molecular graph networks, helping researchers choose operator choices that improve property prediction without relying on costly architecture search.
method: Factorized 2D molecular MPNNs into three operator families: message-seed initialization, node-edge fusion, and node update.
Benchmarked 84 operator combinations on 10 MoleculeNet datasets under a shared protocol.
Used statistical analysis and a Quinethazone representation probe to compare family-level effects and oversmoothing behavior.
evidence_signal: Across 84 configurations and 10 MoleculeNet datasets, the benchmark found significant effects for message-seed initialization and regression-level node-edge fusion, while node update showed no statistically supported effect.
watch_item: The conclusions come from a controlled benchmark on 2D molecular MPNNs, so results may not transfer directly to other graph domains, 3D models, or different training regimes.
paper_title: What drives performance in molecular MPNNs? An operator-level factorial benchmark
